message:
fix(mux-server): propagate daemonize intermediate child exit status to grandparent

Three-pass audit of crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/ (main.rs,
daemonize.rs, ossl.rs — 808 lines total):

  1. unwrap/expect on startup paths: clean. Every .unwrap / .expect
     site in the prod zone (pre-test-module at main.rs:308) is either
     an `unwrap_or_else` fallback or test-only; the daemonize and
     ossl files contain none.

  2. missing timeout on blocking ops: no blocking I/O in the crate's
     own prod zone. `spawn_listener` hands each listener off to a
     fresh `std::thread::spawn` and returns; the per-listener run()
     loop is owned by mux-server-impl, which is a separate pane's
     surface.

  3. panic paths in arg parsing: clean. Clap's derived `Opt::parse()`
     handles all arg errors via clap's own error machinery; the
     crate defines no custom value_parser that could panic; the
     referenced `name_equals_value` helper lives in the external
     `wezterm_gui_subcommands` crate.

Defect found in the daemonize flow and fixed.

`crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/daemonize.rs:83-90` had the
canonical double-fork-daemon bug: the outer fork's parent called
`waitpid` on the intermediate child, but silently ignored BOTH the
waitpid return value AND the status word:

    match fork()? {
        Fork::Parent(pid) => {
            let mut status = 0;
            unsafe { libc::waitpid(pid, &mut status, 0) };   // unchecked
            std::process::exit(0);                            // unconditional
        }
        Fork::Child(_) => {}
    }

Consequence: anything that could fail in the intermediate child's
post-fork setup — setsid, the second fork, pidfile locking / write,
the three dup2 stdio redirects — reached the grandparent as a silent
exit(0). The invoking shell (or systemd / supervisord / whatever
wrapped `frankenterm-mux-server --daemonize`) saw success and went
on, but no actual daemon was running. The only visible failure mode
was "user connects to the mux socket and gets ECONNREFUSED".

Fix: decode the waitpid result and propagate the child's exit status
to the grandparent.

  * If `waitpid(pid)` itself errors (-1), print the errno to stderr
    and exit with code 1.
  * If the child exited normally (`libc::WIFEXITED`), exit with the
    child's `libc::WEXITSTATUS`. A failing daemonize now shows up as
    a non-zero exit on the grandparent, surfacing upstream to
    systemd / wrapper scripts as a normal "unit failed" signal.
  * If the child was killed by a signal (`libc::WIFSIGNALED`), log
    the signal number and exit with the conventional `128 + sig`
    encoding.
  * Any other status (e.g. `WIFSTOPPED` — shouldn't happen without
    `WUNTRACED` but defensive), exit with code 1.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server` finishes
clean in 1m 03s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
